item.title:
IRAN BUILDING UP DEFENSES OF KHARG ISLAND TO PROTECT AGAINST POTENTIAL US GROUND ATTACK, SOURCES SAY - CNN
IRAN IS FORTIFYING KHARG ISLAND WITH MINES AND AIR DEFENSES AGAINST POTENTIAL US MILITARY SEIZURE
KHARG ISLAND PROCESSES ROUGHLY 90% OF IRAN’S CRUDE OIL EXPORTS, CRITICAL TO GLOBAL ENERGY MARKETS

item.title:
RECESSION RISKS RISE AS WAR HITS U.S. ECONOMY
Wall Street is raising recession risks as the Iran war pushes up oil prices and weakens the outlook.
Goldman Sachs now sees a 30% recession risk, with inflation near 3% and unemployment rising to 4.6% by 2026. Higher fuel costs—gas up over 30% to ~$4—are squeezing consumers and offsetting tax-cut stimulus.
Firms like Morgan Stanley have cut growth forecasts as spending slows, while economists warn the damage could linger even if the conflict ends soon.
The U.S. economy is still expected to grow around 2%, supported by AI and data center investment, but rising energy, food, and transport costs threaten to drag on hiring and consumer demand.
item.description:
RECESSION RISKS RISE AS WAR HITS U.S. ECONOMY

Wall Street is raising recession risks as the Iran war pushes up oil prices and weakens the outlook.

Goldman Sachs now sees a 30% recession risk, with inflation near 3% and unemployment rising to 4.6% by 2026. Higher fuel costs—gas up over 30% to ~$4—are squeezing consumers and offsetting tax-cut stimulus.

Firms like Morgan Stanley have cut growth forecasts as spending slows, while economists warn the damage could linger even if the conflict ends soon.

The U.S. economy is still expected to grow around 2%, supported by AI and data center investment, but rising energy, food, and transport costs threaten to drag on hiring and consumer demand.

item.title:
BENTLEY KYIV RANKS 3RD IN THE WORLD
Bentley Kyiv has achieved 3rd place out of 61 Bentley dealerships worldwide in the prestigious “Best of the Best” nomination, according to the Business Performance Matrix.
The average price of a Bentley starts at $400,000.
item.description:

Bentley Kyiv has achieved 3rd place out of 61 Bentley dealerships worldwide in the prestigious “Best of the Best” nomination, according to the Business Performance Matrix.

The average price of a Bentley starts at $400,000.

item.title:
MARKETS HOLD STRONG AS MIDDLE EAST WAR DRAGS ON
Deutsche Bank strategist Henry Allen says markets haven’t faced a 2022-style shock yet despite stagflation fears from the Middle East conflict.
Three key reasons: oil prices remain moderate (Brent ~$84/bbl vs. $100+ in 2022), major central banks haven’t tightened policy, and early economic data in the U.S., Eurozone, and U.K. still shows growth.
Allen notes historical patterns suggest equities could weaken broadly if the shock deepens, though energy and countries with low inflation may fare better.
item.description:
MARKETS HOLD STRONG AS MIDDLE EAST WAR DRAGS ON

Deutsche Bank strategist Henry Allen says markets haven’t faced a 2022-style shock yet despite stagflation fears from the Middle East conflict.

Three key reasons: oil prices remain moderate (Brent ~$84/bbl vs. $100+ in 2022), major central banks haven’t tightened policy, and early economic data in the U.S., Eurozone, and U.K. still shows growth.

Allen notes historical patterns suggest equities could weaken broadly if the shock deepens, though energy and countries with low inflation may fare better.

item.title:
IRAQ OIL OUTPUT COLLAPSES AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION
Iraq has slashed oil production by about 80%—down to roughly 800,000 barrels per day—as storage fills and exports through the Strait of Hormuz remain blocked by conflict.
Baghdad has ordered further cuts, instructing majors like BP and Eni to reduce output at key southern fields.
Officials warn production could fall even more if disruptions persist.
item.description:
IRAQ OIL OUTPUT COLLAPSES AMID HORMUZ DISRUPTION

Iraq has slashed oil production by about 80%—down to roughly 800,000 barrels per day—as storage fills and exports through the Strait of Hormuz remain blocked by conflict.

Baghdad has ordered further cuts, instructing majors like BP and Eni to reduce output at key southern fields.

Officials warn production could fall even more if disruptions persist.

item.title: HEZBOLLAH CHIEF: FIGHTERS DETERMINED TO CONTINUE WITHOUT LIMITS
item.description: HEZBOLLAH CHIEF: FIGHTERS DETERMINED TO CONTINUE WITHOUT LIMITS

item.title:
BITCOIN RISES AS MARKET FEAR EASES
Bitcoin is climbing, up 5.4% over the past month to around $71,400, as market sentiment stabilizes.
CoinMarketCap’s Fear & Greed Index has improved to 37—still in “fear” territory, but well above last month’s extreme lows, signaling reduced panic.
Analysts see potential short-term upside, supported by easing geopolitical tensions and improving investor confidence.
item.description:
BITCOIN RISES AS MARKET FEAR EASES

Bitcoin is climbing, up 5.4% over the past month to around $71,400, as market sentiment stabilizes.

CoinMarketCap’s Fear & Greed Index has improved to 37—still in “fear” territory, but well above last month’s extreme lows, signaling reduced panic.

Analysts see potential short-term upside, supported by easing geopolitical tensions and improving investor confidence.
